citations.narrative:
The following areas of the outdoor facility housing several free-roaming duck species were in disrepair.
-upper pelican/island area: Inside the large opening under a rock feature there was an approximately 1 ft. section of a
loose, detached wire mesh screen with exposed, sharp edges.
- paradise falls/chapel area: There were three, approximately 1ft.X1ft cut-out, surface openings on the side of a rock
feature. The spaces behind the openings appear to have created a deep cavity.
- mermaid pond: There was an approximately 2ft.X1ft. opening/hole along the top surface of a large rock feature adjacent
to the pond.
-first island: There was a large surface crack along one of the sides of the rocks approximately 1in. wide and 1.5ft long.
-back garden area: There was an approximately 6ft. section of plastic fencing separating the pond from the back garden
area lying loosely folded on the ground.
Birds may potentially become trapped or injured in these openings/holes and cracks in the rocks or in the loose fencing
material within their enclosure. Additionally, any sharp edges on wire material may also cause injury to the animals.
Primary enclosures must be constructed and maintained in good repair so that they protect the birds from injury.
Correct by 02/27/2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
n

narrative:
3.153(a)(1)(ii) 
   
Primary enclosures. 
The following areas of the outdoor facility housing several free-roaming duck species were in disrepair. 
 
-upper pelican/island area: Inside the large opening under a rock feature there was an approximately 1 ft. section of a 
loose, detached wire mesh screen with exposed, sharp edges. 
- paradise falls/chapel area: There were three, approximately 1ft.X1ft cut-out, surface openings on the side of a rock 
feature. The spaces behind the openings appear to have created a deep cavity.  
- mermaid pond: There was an approximately 2ft.X1ft. opening/hole along the top surface of a large rock feature adjacent 
to the pond. 
-first island: There was a large surface crack along one of the sides of the rocks approximately 1in. wide and 1.5ft long.  
-back garden area: There was an approximately 6ft. section of plastic fencing separating the pond from the back garden 
area lying loosely folded on the ground. 
 
Birds may potentially become trapped or injured in these openings/holes and cracks in the rocks or in the loose fencing 
material within their enclosure. Additionally, any sharp edges on wire material may also cause injury to the animals. 
Primary enclosures must be constructed and maintained in good repair so that they protect the birds from injury. 
 
Correct by 02/27/2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
